analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 9 finding(s) from 10 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: Top-level JTAG/DTM debug requests are connected to the debug module without a visible top-level jtag_unlock/auth gate; simulation DTM path also drives DMI directly when compiled in.
findings.vulnerability_category: issue DMI/debug request operations that can activate debug module reset/debug-entry/control state / dm_top outputs ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, debug_req_o and debug-module slave/master state / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external JTAG DTM or simulation DTM requester Operation: issue DMI/debug request operations that can activate debug module reset/debug-entry/control state Object: dm_top outputs ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, debug_req_o and debug-module slave/master state Expected guard: debug authorization/unlock such as jtag_unlock must mediate DMI/debug requests before debug entry, ndmreset, dmactive, or protected state access Observed behavior: dmi_jtag produces jtag_unlock and also directly drives debug_req/debug_req_valid toward the debug module. The visible top level does not gate debug_req_valid/debug_req with jtag_unlock; jtag_unlock is visibly passed to reglk_wrapper. Under RISCV_FESVR_SIM, SimDTM drives debug_req_valid/debug_req without visible unlock mediation. Path: tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i or SimDTM -> dmi_jtag/SimDTM -> debug_req/debug_req_valid -> dm_top -> ndmreset_o/dmactive_o/debug_req_o Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If dmi_jtag or SimDTM does not fully enforce authorization internally, an unauthenticated requester could activate debug, request hart debug entry, or assert non-debug-module reset.

findings.summary: Access-control decode aliases peripheral index 5 with index 4, allowing index-4 permission to satisfy the index-5 guard used by PKT/fuse path.
findings.vulnerability_category: route/read/write MMIO requests to protected peripheral wrappers / peripheral request paths including ROM, reset, crypto/RNG/HMAC/PKT, ACCT, REGLK, and DMA endpoints / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: CPU/NoC MMIO requester selected by priv_lvl_i Operation: route/read/write MMIO requests to protected peripheral wrappers Object: peripheral request paths including ROM, reset, crypto/RNG/HMAC/PKT, ACCT, REGLK, and DMA endpoints Expected guard: each peripheral access must be gated by intended acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][target] bit without unsafe aliasing between peripheral indices Observed behavior: acc_ctrl_c[i][j] is assigned acc_ctrl[j*4+i] | (j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i]). Thus for target j==5, access is granted by either the target bit or the bit for index 4. PKT wrapper uses acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][5] and exposes fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata. Path: NoC AXI-lite bridge -> peripheral wrapper axi_req_i; acc_ctrl -> acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][target] -> wrapper acct_ctrl_i; special OR alias for j==5 Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A requester authorized for peripheral index 4 may reach peripheral index 5 (PKT/fuse-related function) without the intended index-5 permission, potentially exposing or manipulating protected packet/fuse-backed functionality.

findings.summary: DMA wrapper receives reglk_ctrl_i but the visible DMA register write block updates configuration registers without referencing reglk_ctrl_i, and core_lock_reg can be cleared by writes/reset.
findings.vulnerability_category: write or reconfigure locked DMA configuration/control registers / DMA start, length, source address, destination address, done/end, and core_lock registers / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software-visible AXI/MMIO requester targeting DMA registers Operation: write or reconfigure locked DMA configuration/control registers Object: DMA start, length, source address, destination address, done/end, and core_lock registers Expected guard: once a lock bit is set, all software/debug/test write paths modifying protected DMA configuration must be blocked until permitted reset/lifecycle event Observed behavior: dma_wrapper gates the AXI-lite enable with acct_ctrl_i, but the write block updates registers on en && we and does not include reglk_ctrl_i. core_lock_reg is writable; expression can clear it when wdata==0 and also clears it when nonzero write occurs while already locked. Path: NoC/AXI-lite -> dma_wrapper axi_lite_interface -> en/we/address/wdata -> DMA register write always block -> dma instance inputs Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A permitted MMIO writer can reconfigure DMA despite asserted register-lock policy, and can clear/rewrite core_lock state, undermining lock/sticky protection.

findings.summary: DMA PMP checking has a we_flag bypass for source checks and destination checks do not visibly accumulate allow over the full destination range.
findings.vulnerability_category: initiate source read and destination write transfer / memory regions addressed by source_addr_*_reg, dest_addr_*_reg, and length_reg / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: DMA engine configured through MMIO Operation: initiate source read and destination write transfer Object: memory regions addressed by source_addr_*_reg, dest_addr_*_reg, and length_reg Expected guard: DMA start and every source/destination address in the transfer must be authorized by PMP/range/access checks before valid transfer state asserts Observed behavior: DMA performs source and destination PMP checks before asserting VALID_LOAD/VALID_STORE, but source check sets pmp_allow_new true on (we_flag && length_d == 3). Destination check assigns pmp_allow_new = pmp_data_allow for each checked address rather than accumulating with prior pmp_allow_reg, so earlier denied destination addresses can be overwritten by later allowed checks before the terminal decision. Path: dma_wrapper MMIO registers -> dma start_i/length_i/source/dest inputs -> dma FSM -> pmp_addr_reg/pmp_access_type_reg -> pmp allow -> valid_o Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: DMA may assert valid load/store for unauthorized ranges: a test/write-enable flag can bypass source PMP for length 3, and destination transfers may be allowed based only on the last checked address rather than the full range.

findings.summary: axi_lite_interface resets combinational outputs to zero by default, latches address_n/address_q and trans_id, starts WRITE on aw_valid, and later uses write data from axi_req_i.w.data. Provided evidence is truncated before full WRITE/READ sequencing, so stale-address or incomplete-handshake behavior cannot be fully assessed.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write downstream register via AXI-lite bridge / downstream CLINT/wrapper registers selected by address_o/en_o/we_o / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI master connected to axi_lite_interface Operation: read/write downstream register via AXI-lite bridge Object: downstream CLINT/wrapper registers selected by address_o/en_o/we_o Expected guard: AXI-lite conversion must preserve valid address/write intent and downstream permission guards; protected registers must not be written due to stale address or incomplete handshake Observed behavior: axi_lite_interface resets combinational outputs to zero by default, latches address_n/address_q and trans_id, starts WRITE on aw_valid, and later uses write data from axi_req_i.w.data. Provided evidence is truncated before full WRITE/READ sequencing, so stale-address or incomplete-handshake behavior cannot be fully assessed. Path: axi_req_i AW/W/AR channels -> axi_lite_interface state/address_q -> address_o/en_o/we_o/data_o -> downstream wrappers such as dma_wrapper Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Security impact is not fully established from visible evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Missing impact and complete AXI-lite WRITE/READ state sequencing; no concrete protected-register stale-address write was established. Missing evidence slots: impact.
recommended_follow_up: Check security-sensitive consequence of the observed behavior for obligation PERM-AXILITE-006.

findings.summary: PMP entry NAPOT/NA4 matching appears to construct an inverted mask, causing range-match semantics to be suspect; M-mode no-match allow is present by design.
findings.vulnerability_category: allow memory access / PMP-protected memory regions / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: CPU/DMA memory requester using PMP decision Operation: allow memory access Object: PMP-protected memory regions Expected guard: PMP allow must be false unless enabled matching entry grants requested access type for requester privilege; address matching must implement TOR/NA4/NAPOT bounds correctly Observed behavior: pmp.sv defaults allow_o=0, iterates entries in priority order, checks access_type, and defaults no-match to allow for M-mode and deny for other modes. pmp_entry.sv TOR logic is explicit. For NA4/NAPOT, it computes mask='1 << size and base=(conf_addr_i << 2) & mask, then compares (addr_i & mask)==base; this appears to use only the size bit instead of the usual mask covering higher address bits, making match_o potentially incorrect. Path: pmp configuration inputs -> pmp_entry match_o -> pmp allow_o -> DMA pmp_data_allow/transfer decision Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Incorrect NA4/NAPOT matching can grant or deny PMP permissions for the wrong address regions; if DMA relies on this allow decision, it can access memory outside intended PMP ranges or be authorized by an unrelated entry.

findings.summary: Top-level declares fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata and connects them only through pkt_wrapper in the supplied evidence. pkt_wrapper is reached from a NoC AXI-lite bridge and receives acct_ctrl_i(acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][5]) and reglk_ctrl slice. Full pkt_wrapper internals and response mux behavior are not supplied, so actual fuse data exposure cannot be confirmed. However index-5 access is affected by ACC-002 aliasing.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/expose or use fuse data / fuse_rdata and fuse-backed security/lifecycle configuration / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software-visible requester or debug/test interface through PKT wrapper Operation: read/expose or use fuse data Object: fuse_rdata and fuse-backed security/lifecycle configuration Expected guard: fuse read/exposure must be limited to authorized subjects and fuse-derived policy must not be readable or overridden by unprivileged/debug/test paths Observed behavior: Top-level declares fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata and connects them only through pkt_wrapper in the supplied evidence. pkt_wrapper is reached from a NoC AXI-lite bridge and receives acct_ctrl_i(acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][5]) and reglk_ctrl slice. Full pkt_wrapper internals and response mux behavior are not supplied, so actual fuse data exposure cannot be confirmed. However index-5 access is affected by ACC-002 aliasing. Path: NoC PKT AXI-lite bridge -> pkt_axi_req/resp -> pkt_wrapper -> fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o/fuse_rdata_i Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Security impact is not fully established from visible evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Missing impact, pkt_wrapper internal fuse read mux/request enable, and external response path proving fuse_rdata exposure. Missing evidence slots: impact.
recommended_follow_up: Check security-sensitive consequence of the observed behavior for obligation PERM-FUSE-008.

findings.summary: Top-level reset wrapper receives reglk_ctrl slice, acct_ctrl_i(acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][12]), debug_mode_i, and produces rst_1..rst_13. The reset NoC bridge exposes this wrapper to NoC requests. dma_wrapper reset condition clears DMA config and core_lock_reg when rst_8 is asserted. The internal rst_wrapper decode/write policy is not supplied, so whether unauthorized subjects can assert rst_8 is not established.
findings.vulnerability_category: trigger peripheral reset or clear security state / rst_1..rst_13 lines and security/lock/configuration state affected by reset, including rst_8 into DMA / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software/NoC/debug requester controlling reset Operation: trigger peripheral reset or clear security state Object: rst_1..rst_13 lines and security/lock/configuration state affected by reset, including rst_8 into DMA Expected guard: writes asserting peripheral resets must be privilege/access-control guarded, and reset must not clear locks/security state in a way permitting unauthorized reconfiguration Observed behavior: Top-level reset wrapper receives reglk_ctrl slice, acct_ctrl_i(acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][12]), debug_mode_i, and produces rst_1..rst_13. The reset NoC bridge exposes this wrapper to NoC requests. dma_wrapper reset condition clears DMA config and core_lock_reg when rst_8 is asserted. The internal rst_wrapper decode/write policy is not supplied, so whether unauthorized subjects can assert rst_8 is not established. Path: NoC reset AXI-lite bridge -> rst_axi_req/resp -> rst_wrapper -> rst_1..rst_13, including rst_8 -> dma_wrapper reset behavior Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Security impact is not fully established from visible evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Missing impact and rst_wrapper internal decode/update logic showing whether unauthorized reset assertion is possible. Missing evidence slots: impact.
recommended_follow_up: Check security-sensitive consequence of the observed behavior for obligation PERM-RESET-009.

findings.summary: test/we_flag inputs participate in security-relevant paths, including dmi_jtag and DMA PMP bypass, without visible lifecycle/test authorization in the supplied scope.
findings.vulnerability_category: override write protection or force protected control state / JTAG debug authorization path, DMA/PMP control state, and protected peripheral lock inputs / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: test/manufacturing override interface Operation: override write protection or force protected control state Object: JTAG debug authorization path, DMA/PMP control state, and protected peripheral lock inputs Expected guard: test/manufacturing override signals must not enable writes, unlocks, resets, or protected operations in normal operation unless gated by explicit test lifecycle/debug authorization Observed behavior: riscv_peripherals exposes testmode_i and we_flag_0..4 inputs. we_flag_4 is passed into dmi_jtag. we_flag_3 is passed into dma_wrapper/dma, where dma CTRL_CHECK_LOAD includes a bypass making pmp_allow_new true when we_flag && length_d == 3. we_flag_1 is ORed into an AES0 reglk_ctrl_i slice, potentially modifying lock inputs. No lifecycle/test authorization gate is visible at these top-level connections. Path: external testmode_i/we_flag_N ports -> dmi_jtag, AES0 lock input, DMA wrapper/dma internal PMP logic Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If controllable in normal operation, we_flag/test paths can bypass DMA PMP source checks, alter effective lock controls, or affect debug JTAG behavior, enabling protected operations without the normal permission path.

global_uncertainty: 3 evidence slot(s) remained incomplete after closure checking.
